title: Lesson Quiz: Risk, Return, and Diversification
description: Assessment of the concept that Balancing risk and reward in investment decisions.

questions.prompt: What does diversification mean in investing?

choices.text: Spreading investments across more than one option
choices.feedback: Correct. Diversification means not depending on one investment alone.

choices.text: Placing all money into one investment
choices.feedback: Putting all money in one option is the opposite of diversification.
Diversification can reduce some risk, but it does not remove all risk.
Diversification is part of investment planning, not a reason to ignore growth.

questions.prompt: Strong investment decisions should compare both possible return and possible risk.

choices.feedback: Strong investment decisions consider both the chance for growth and the possibility of loss.
Correct. Risk and return should be evaluated together.

questions.prompt: Why can diversification be a stronger strategy than relying on one investment only?

choices.text: Because it can reduce the risk of depending on one result
choices.feedback: This is the strongest reason, but the correct answer in this set is placed elsewhere.

choices.text: Because it guarantees that no investment will ever lose value
choices.feedback: Diversification can reduce some risk, but it does not guarantee no loss.

choices.text: Because it can reduce the risk of depending on one result
choices.feedback: Correct. Diversification spreads risk instead of concentrating it in one choice.

choices.text: Because it removes the need to compare investments
choices.feedback: Investment options still need to be compared carefully.

questions.prompt: Which investment pattern is more diversified?

choices.text: All money placed in one single investment
choices.feedback: That pattern depends on one option and is less diversified.
This is the strongest answer, but the correct answer in this set is placed elsewhere.

choices.text: All money kept in one risky choice because it might grow faster
choices.feedback: That pattern is more concentrated, not more diversified.
Correct. Spreading investments across more than one option is more diversified.

questions.prompt: A higher possible return can come with higher risk.

choices.feedback: Correct. Higher possible return can be connected to higher risk.
Investment decisions often involve a trade-off between risk and possible return.
